LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY INC.'S ("DWC") MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DWC WAS PERFORMING EXCAVATION WORK IN THE ACCIDENT AREA THE DAY BEFORE THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED AND WHETHER THAT WORK CAUSED MR. SANCHEZ'S INJURY. (BELL DEC., PARS. 5-6; SANCHEZ DEPO., 39-40.) (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. Off Calendar May-22-2018 Continued To Feb-11-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. John Henning of the Law offices of Brian L. Larsen, Tel. No. 1-415-398-5000
specially appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Matthew H. Weiner of Lorber, Greenfield & Polito, Tel. No. 1-415-986-0688
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Doyle's Work Company Inc.'s ("DWC") motion for summary judgment is DENIED. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether DWC was performing excavation work in the accident area the day before the accident occurred and whether that work caused Mr. Sanchez's injury. (Bell Dec., pars. 5-6; Sanchez Depo., 39-40.) The court overrules DWC's objections to plaintiff's testimony as irrelevant, speculative and lacking foundation. Privette v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689, and its progeny does not bar liability as Mr. Sanchez creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether DWC was directly or affirmatively negligent. Put simply, if DWC was conducting excavation work in the area where Mr. Sanchez was working, DWC owed a duty to perform that work reasonably. The agreement between DWC and DPR does not bar Mr. Sanchez's claim. Even if DPR assumed all excavation safety responsibilities, which overstates the terms of the agreement, DWC does not cite any authority holding that such an agreement would bar Mr. Sanchez's claims. That appears to be a claim of contractual indemnity between DWC and DPR per Labor Code sec. 3864. The court does not find TIC Energy and Chemical, Inc. v. Martin (2016) 498 S.W.3d 68 apposite as that case relates to application of a particular Texas statute. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600
